hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming favorable π-π stacking interactions with ECL2 (residue 192/193/195/197) in SSTR2, which are absent in off-target subtypes. Mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will introduce electrostatic complementarity with SSTR2-specific TM5 (residue 205/208/209/212), while disrupting off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is valid — selectivity is achievable with Δmargin > 0. However, the structural quality of the designs is a critical bottleneck. The next iteration should focus on improving structural confidence while maintaining selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Current objective mode prioritizes ddG alone, leading to candidates with strong binding but poor structural confidence. Adding pLDDT as a secondary objective will enforce higher structural quality.

The low pass rate (12.5%) suggests the search space is too constrained. Increasing the number of candidates will diversify the designs and improve the chances of finding high-quality, high-confidence structures.
